name: From the mountains to the sea with the Gravelbike
name_original: Von den Bergen bis zum Meer mit dem Gravelbike
description:
The green lung of Barcelona rises behind the streets and houses of the city: the Serra de Collserola Natural Park. Covering more than 8,000 hectares, it offers city dwellers an expansive space for walking, cycling and relaxing from the hustle and bustle of the city.

On this tour you drive along the Carretera de les Aigues along the very edge of the park - you always have magnificent views down to the city and the sea on the horizon. At the very end, a sea bath at the Platja de la Mar Bella lures.

With only 40 kilometers, you can also do the tour in one afternoon. It starts from the Avinguda Diagonal straight ahead and past the Zona Universitaria. Then you zigzag uphill on a bike path. You reach the entrance to Collserola Park and can look forward to kilometers of gravel happiness with an outstanding panorama of Barcelona.

Since the route is also used by people on foot, you should adjust your speed on the bike accordingly. In several places you have the opportunity to pause and enjoy the great view in peace - just the right thing to escape the hustle and bustle of the big city for a moment.

You slowly make your way downhill again. A ten-kilometer descent beckons, which is just plain fun. If you keep going straight through Vilapiscina, el Clot and Poble Nou, you will get straight to the Platja de Mar Bella. A dip in the sea is the perfect end to your little trip. How awesome is it to live in a city nestled between the mountains and the sea?
region_intro: Gravel biking around Barcelones offers a diverse landscape for exploration, situated between the Mediterranean Sea and various mountain ranges. The region features accessible natural parks like Serra de Collserola and Serralada de Marina, providing numerous gravel roads and trails. Riders can experience varied terrain, from the flat, wetland paths of the Llobregat Delta to the challenging climbs within the coastal hills. This area combines urban proximity with natural beauty, making it suitable for a range of gravel biking experiences. Best gravel bike trails around Barcelones The most popular gravel bike trail is From the mountains to the sea with the Gravelbike, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route offers a journey from elevated terrain down to the coast. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Badalona & Parc Serralada Marina with the Gravelbike, a difficult 30.8 miles (49.5 km) path. This trail explores the varied landscapes of Parc Serralada Marina, including Mediterranean forests and coastal views. Local gravel bikers also love the Delta del Llobregat & Montjuïc With the Gravelbike, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail leading through the Llobregat Delta and past Montjuïc, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Gravel biking around Barcelones is defined by coastal plains, Mediterranean forests, and river deltas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Barcelones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 5,000 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Barcelones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Barcelones?
A: Barcelones offers a wide variety of gravel biking experiences, with over 110 distinct routes available. These range from easy paths to more challenging rides, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Barcelones?
A: The gravel bike trails in Barcelones are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 5,000 gravel bikers have explored the region, often praising the diverse terrain and stunning views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Barcelones?
A: Gravel biking around Barcelones offers incredibly diverse terrain. You'll find everything from the flat, wetland paths of the Llobregat Delta to the challenging climbs within the coastal hills of Serra de Collserola and Serralada de Marina. Expect a mix of Mediterranean forests, riverbanks, and open landscapes.

Q: Are there gravel bike trails suitable for beginners in Barcelones?
A: Yes, Barcelones has options for beginners. While many routes are more challenging, there are at least 2 easy gravel bike trails available. The region's diverse landscape, including areas like the Llobregat Delta, offers flatter sections ideal for those new to gravel biking.

Q: What are some challenging gravel bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Barcelones offers numerous difficult routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e564838057/badalona-parc-serralada-marina-with-the-gravelbike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Badalona & Parc Serralada Marina with the Gravelbike&lt;/a&gt;, a 49.5 km path exploring varied landscapes. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933299245/through-the-parc-de-collserola-to-la-floresta-by-gravelbike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Through the Parc de Collserola to La Floresta By Gravelbike&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park.

Q: How can I access gravel bike trails in Barcelones using public transport?
A: Many areas, especially those within Serra de Collserola Natural Park, are easily accessible from Barcelona by public transport. For example, the Carretera de les Aigües, a popular gravel path, can be reached via FGC trains or buses that stop near the park's entrances. The Llobregat Delta area is also accessible by public transport from the city.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes near Barcelona?
A: Yes, several circular gravel routes are available. The Penedès region, an hour south of Barcelona, offers the 'Penedès 360 Original,' a 162 km circular gravel route. Within Barcelones, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31157716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collserola Tower – Carretera de les Aigües loop from Verdaguer&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de les Aigües – Carretera de les Aigües loop from Hospital Clínic&lt;/a&gt; provide excellent circular options within Serra de Collserola.

Q: Can I bring my dog on gravel bike trails in Barcelones?
A: Many natural parks and rural areas around Barcelones are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. It's always best to check specific park regulations or individual trail rules before heading out with your dog.

Q: Are there family-friendly gravel biking options in Barcelones?
A: For families, the flatter sections of the Llobregat Delta offer long, easy paths ideal for a relaxed ride. The Carretera de les Aigües in Serra de Collserola is also a relatively easy and popular option with stunning views, suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with gentle inclines.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see along the gravel bike trails?
A: Many trails offer breathtaking views. In Serra de Collserola, you can enjoy panoramic views of Barcelona and the sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cypresses Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miramar Gardens&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934021910/delta-del-llobregat-montjuic-with-the-gravelbike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Delta del Llobregat & Montjuïc With the Gravelbike&lt;/a&gt; route passes by Montjuïc, where you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3976347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magic Fountain of Montjuïc&lt;/a&gt;. The Parc del Garraf also provides unique viewpoints over its arid landscape.

Q: Which natural parks are best for gravel biking around Barcelones?
A: The most prominent natural parks for gravel biking are Serra de Collserola, directly above Barcelona, offering numerous trails and views. Serralada de Marina, to the right of Barcelona, also provides varied terrain. Further afield, Montseny Natural Park and Garraf Natural Park offer distinct landscapes and challenging routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Barcelones?
A: Barcelones benefits from a mild climate for much of the year, making it ideal for cycling almost year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Even winter can be enjoyable, though summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Where can I find parking for gravel biking in areas like Collserola?
A: For routes in Serra de Collserola, parking is available at various access points around the park's perimeter. Specific trailheads often have designated parking areas, particularly near popular starting points for routes like the Carretera de les Aigües. It's advisable to check local parking regulations before your visit.
tips: Here you will find all the necessary tools to fix your bike. You can also inflate your tires. Super practical!

A truly fantastic road that is equally exciting on foot or by bike! You ride here on fine gravel for kilometers on gently undulating terrain and always have an outstanding panoramic view of Barcelona and the ocean at your side. You can hardly imagine a nicer break from hectic city life.
A nice long bench - treat yourself to a break and enjoy the wide panorama to Besòs and the ocean.
Spectacular views of Barcelona from this crossroads called Forat del Vent.
